task_completion.description: Python 3.x versions (3.0 through 3.13) and their dates are added as milestone markers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io canvas for new milestone markers/nodes labeled with Python 3.x versions (e.g., 3.0, 3.6, 3.11, 3.13) and their corresponding release dates.

task_completion.description: Key features are included as text annotations for the releases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text boxes or labels near the version milestones containing feature names (e.g., 'asyncio', 'f-strings', 'Walrus operator', 'pattern matching').

task_completion.description: Timeline is organized into three distinct phase containers with unique background colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for three large background shapes or grouping containers enclosing the milestones. They must be labeled 'Python 2 Era', 'Transition Period', and 'Modern Python 3', and each must have a different background color.

task_completion.description: A visible text label 'EOL: Jan 1, 2020' is added near the Python 2.7 milestone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact text 'EOL: Jan 1, 2020' positioned next to or pointing to the Python 2.7 milestone marker.

task_completion.description: The diagram is exported as a PDF to ~/Diagrams/exports/python_timeline.pdf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io 'Export as PDF' dialog, a file manager/terminal showing 'python_timeline.pdf' in the '~/Diagrams/exports/' directory, or the final PDF opened in a viewer.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io (diagrams.net) graphical interface to edit the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the draw.io application open with the timeline diagram being actively edited, rather than just a text editor modifying XML.

integrity.description: The PDF was generated from the diagram rather than being a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with the draw.io File -> Export as -> PDF menu, or the resulting PDF visually matching the edited diagram.

integrity.description: The agent opened or referenced the python_releases.txt file to get the correct data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of '~/Desktop/python_releases.txt' during the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The timeline must include Python 3.0 through 3.13. Key features to look for in annotations include: 'asyncio' (3.4), 'f-strings' (3.6), 'Walrus operator :=' (3.8), and 'Structural pattern matching' (3.10). The three phase containers must be named exactly 'Python 2 Era', 'Transition Period', and 'Modern Python 3'. The Python 2.7 EOL label must state 'Jan 1, 2020' (or 'January 1, 2020').
